content:
# Numbers Chapter 32 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 188-190 | **Source Image:** `NUM_032_page_001.jpg`, `NUM_032_page_002.jpg`, `NUM_032_page_003.jpg`
**Secondary Source:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. pa_0138-pa_0139

**1.** And the sons of Reuben and the sons of Gad had a very great multitude of livestock. And they saw the land of Jazer and the land of Gilead, and behold, the place was a place for livestock.

**2.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben came and spoke to Moses and to Eleazar the priest and to the leaders of the congregation, saying,

**3.** "Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Sebam, and Nebo, and Beon —

**4.** the land which YHWH struck before the congregation of Israel — it is a land for livestock, and your servants have livestock."

**5.** And they said, "If we have found favor in your eyes, let this land be given to your servants as a possession; do not make us cross over the Jordan."

**6.** And Moses said to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben, "Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?

**7.** And why do you discourage the heart of the sons of Israel from crossing over into the land which YHWH has given to them?

**8.** Thus your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land.

**9.** And they went up to the Valley of Eshcol and saw the land, and they discouraged the heart of the sons of Israel so that they did not go into the land which YHWH had given to them.

**10.** And the anger of YHWH was kindled on that day, and he swore, saying,

**11.** 'Surely none of the men who came up from Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land which I swore to Abraham, to Isaac, and to Jacob, for they did not wholly follow after me —

**12.** except Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua the son of Nun, for they wholly followed after YHWH.'

**13.** And the anger of YHWH was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until all the generation that had done evil in the eyes of YHWH was consumed.

**14.** And behold, you have risen up in your fathers' place, a brood of sinful men, to add still more to the burning anger of YHWH against Israel.

**15.** For if you turn away from following him, he will once again leave them in the wilderness, and you will destroy all this people."

**16.** And they drew near to him and said, "We will build sheepfolds here for our livestock and cities for our little ones.

**17.** But we ourselves will be armed, ready to go before the sons of Israel, until we have brought them to their place. And our little ones shall dwell in the fortified cities because of the inhabitants of the land.

**18.** We will not return to our houses until each one of the sons of Israel has inherited his inheritance.

**19.** For we will not inherit with them on the other side of the Jordan and beyond, for our inheritance has come to us on the east side of the Jordan."

**20.** And Moses said to them, "If you will do this thing — if you will arm yourselves before YHWH for the war,

**21.** and every armed man of you will cross over the Jordan before YHWH until he has driven out his enemies from before him,

**22.** and the land is subdued before YHWH — then afterward you may return and be free of obligation before YHWH and before Israel, and this land shall be your possession before YHWH.

**23.** But if you will not do so, behold, you will have sinned against YHWH, and know that your sin will find you out.

**24.** Build for yourselves cities for your little ones and folds for your flocks, and do what has gone out of your mouth."

**25.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben spoke to Moses, saying, "Your servants will do as my lord commands.

**26.** Our little ones, our wives, our livestock, and all our cattle shall remain there in the cities of Gilead.

**27.** But your servants will cross over, every man armed for war, before YHWH to battle, as my lord speaks."

**28.** And Moses commanded concerning them Eleazar the priest and Joshua the son of Nun and the heads of the fathers of the tribes of the sons of Israel.

**29.** And Moses said to them, "If the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben cross over with you the Jordan, every man armed for war before YHWH, and the land is subdued before you, then you shall give to them the land of Gilead as a possession.

**30.** But if they do not cross over armed with you, they shall receive possessions among you in the land of Canaan."

**31.** And the sons of Gad and the sons of Reuben answered, saying, "What YHWH has spoken to your servants, so we will do.

**32.** We ourselves will cross over armed before YHWH into the land of Canaan, and the possession of our inheritance shall be with us on this side of the Jordan."

**33.** And Moses gave to them — to the sons of Gad and to the sons of Reuben and to the half-tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph — the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land with its cities within the borders, the cities of the land all around.

**34.** And the sons of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,

**35.** and Atroth-shophan, and Jazer, and Jogbehah,

**36.** and Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran — fortified cities and folds for flocks.

**37.** And the sons of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kiriathaim,

**38.** and Nebo, and Baal-meon — their names being changed — and Sibmah; and they gave other names to the cities which they built.

**39.** And the sons of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead and captured it, and they dispossessed the Amorites who were in it.

**40.** And Moses gave Gilead to Machir the son of Manasseh, and he dwelt in it.

**41.** And Jair the son of Manasseh went and captured their villages, and he called them Havvoth-jair.

**42.** And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its daughters, and he called it Nobah, after his own name.

---
## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Textual Notes
- **v. 1:** Hebrew מִקְנֶה רַב ("a great multitude of livestock"). The Leningrad Codex reads וּמִקְנֶה רַב עָצוּם, literally "and livestock great, mighty." The adjective עָצוּם intensifies the quantity. The LXX (Vaticanus) renders κτήνη πολλά ("many cattle"), omitting the second intensifier.
- **v. 3:** The city list follows the Masoretic sequence. "Sebam" (שְׂבָם) appears as "Sibmah" (שִׂבְמָה) in v. 38, likely variant spellings of the same place. "Nimrah" (נִמְרָה) is the shortened form of "Beth-nimrah" (בֵּית נִמְרָה) in v. 36.
- **v. 7:** Hebrew תְנִיאוּן (hiphil of נוא), "to discourage, dissuade, hinder." Rendered "discourage the heart of" to preserve the Hebrew idiom with לֵב ("heart").
- **v. 12:** "Kenizzite" (הַקְּנִזִּי) — a clan designation for Caleb, indicating his non-native Israelite lineage (cf. Gen 15:19; Josh 14:6, 14).
- **v. 14:** Hebrew תַּרְבּוּת אֲנָשִׁים חַטָּאִים, literally "an increase of sinful men." The term תַּרְבּוּת ("brood, increase") carries the sense of offspring who multiply the same transgression as their fathers. Rendered "brood" for its connotation of both offspring and proliferation.
- **v. 17:** Hebrew וַאֲנַחְנוּ נֵחָלֵץ חֻשִׁים, "and we ourselves will arm ourselves, hastening." The niphal נֵחָלֵץ conveys reflexive arming, while חֻשִׁים adds urgency ("in haste"). Rendered "armed, ready to go" to capture both elements.
- **v. 22:** Hebrew וִהְיִיתֶם נְקִיִּם — "and you shall be clean/free of obligation." The adjective נָקִי carries legal force: acquitted, guiltless, free from covenant duty. Rendered "free of obligation."
- **v. 23:** The phrase וּדְעוּ חַטַּאתְכֶם אֲשֶׁר תִּמְצָא אֶתְכֶם ("and know your sin which will find you") is a proverbial formulation. Sin is personified as a pursuer — a striking rhetorical device in Moses' speech.
- **v. 33:** The half-tribe of Manasseh is introduced here for the first time in this narrative without prior dialogue. This has prompted much scholarly discussion; the Masoretic text presents it as Moses' initiative.
- **v. 38:** Hebrew מוּסַבֹּת שֵׁם, literally "turned around as to name," meaning their names were changed (presumably to remove pagan theophoric elements such as "Baal" and "Nebo"). The LXX reads περικεκυκλωμένας, interpreting it as "surrounded" (a geographic sense), diverging from the MT's naming context.
- **v. 41:** "Havvoth-jair" (חַוֹּת יָאִיר) — literally "the tent-villages of Jair." The noun חַוָּה refers to small unwalled settlements or encampments.
- **v. 42:** "Kenath and its daughters" (קְנָת וְאֶת־בְּנֹתֶיהָ) — "daughters" (בָּנוֹת) is a standard Hebrew term for satellite towns or dependent villages surrounding a principal city.

### Translation Philosophy
This translation maintains formal equivalence with the Leningrad Codex. Hebrew syntax is preserved as much as possible in readable English, including waw-consecutive constructions rendered as "And..." at the start of verses, and maintaining the Hebrew speech-introduction formulae ("And X spoke to Y, saying"). Place names follow standard English transliterations of the Hebrew forms.
